target.title: fix(opencode-go): load context windows from bundled catalog
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes the `opencode-go` context-window fallback that left models outside the tiny manifest subset at the default 200K sync budget.
- Moves `opencode-go` model metadata to a single source of truth by rebuilding the runtime catalog from `extensions/opencode-go/openclaw.plugin.json` instead of a separate hard-coded TS list.
- Lets the sync `resolveContextTokensForModel()` path consult bundled `refreshable` manifest rows after config/cache misses, so existing configured users get the right compaction threshold without re-onboarding.
- Keeps onboarding aligned by making `applyOpencodeGoProviderConfig()` write the same catalog rows into config defaults.

This matters now because issue #92912 is a real session-state regression: memory flush and preflight compaction are using `DEFAULT_CONTEXT_TOKENS` for several `opencode-go` models, so 1M-window models compact around ~156K instead of ~956K.

Intended outcome: `opencode-go` sync callers resolve the provider-owned context window for models like `mimo-v2.5-pro` and `deepseek-v4-pro`, even before async discovery warms caches.

Out of scope: broad provider-runtime catalog refactors, runtime discovery changes for unrelated providers, or changing the conservative bare-id cache semantics.

Success looks like: the bundled catalog contains the full OpenCode Go model set, sync context lookup can read those rows, and memory-flush callers stop falling back to 200K for these models.

Reviewers should focus on the sync fallback order in `src/agents/context.ts`, the `refreshable` manifest-catalog read in `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.static-catalog.ts`, and the `opencode-go` manifest/runtime catalog unification.

## Linked context

Closes #92912

Related #92913

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Yes. This PR was created in response to a maintainer request to open a fresh branch and land the issue fix with a PR body that satisfies the Real behavior proof gate.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:
  `opencode-go` models outside the tiny manifest subset were missing from the sync context-window path, so memory flush and compaction callers fell back to `200000` instead of provider-owned manifest values.
- Real environment tested:
  Local source checkout on macOS against the actual `openclaw` repo working tree, using the bundled `opencode-go` manifest and current source files.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  `node --input-type=module` scripts run from the repo root to read the current `extensions/opencode-go/openclaw.plugin.json` and print the effective post-fix rows for `deepseek-v4-pro`, `mimo-v2.5-pro`, `kimi-k2.7-code`, and `minimax-m3`, plus a before/after comparison against `origin/main`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  Terminal capture after this patch:
  ```json
  {
    "modelCount": 13,
    "rows": {
      "deepseek-v4-pro": {
        "contextWindow": 1000000,
        "maxTokens": 384000,
        "api": "openai-completions",
        "baseUrl": "https://opencode.ai/zen/go/v1"
      },
      "kimi-k2.7-code": {
        "contextWindow": 262144,
        "maxTokens": 262144,
        "api": "openai-completions",
        "baseUrl": "https://opencode.ai/zen/go/v1"
      },
      "mimo-v2.5-pro": {
        "contextWindow": 1048576,
        "maxTokens": 131072,
        "api": "openai-completions",
        "baseUrl": "https://opencode.ai/zen/go/v1"
      },
      "minimax-m3": {
        "contextWindow": 512000,
        "maxTokens": 128000,
        "api": "anthropic-messages",
        "baseUrl": "https://opencode.ai/zen/go"
      }
    }
  }
  ```
  Before/after manifest coverage against `origin/main`:
  ```json
  {
    "beforeCount": 2,
    "afterCount": 13,
    "added": [
      "glm-5",
      "glm-5.1",
      "kimi-k2.7-code",
      "kimi-k2.6",
      "mimo-v2.5",
      "mimo-v2.5-pro",
      "minimax-m2.7",
      "minimax-m3",
      "qwen3.6-plus",
      "qwen3.7-max",
      "qwen3.7-plus"
    ]
  }
  ```
- Observed result after fix:
  The bundled OpenCode Go catalog now exposes the 13-model list requested by the user, and the sync fallback path can source those values from bundled `refreshable` manifest metadata instead of dropping to `200000`.
- What was not tested:
  I could not complete local Vitest or full CLI runtime execution in this checkout because the current environment is missing linked dev/runtime packages (`vitest`, `esbuild`, `json5`, and `@openclaw/*` module resolution), and the available Crabbox binary is too old for current Testbox proof.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints:
  Local `scripts/run-vitest.mjs` and `pnpm test ...` both fail early with `OPENCLAW_MISSING_VITEST`; `pnpm build` fails on missing `esbuild`; `node openclaw.mjs ...` fails on missing `json5`; and `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox ...` fails because the installed Crabbox binary is `0.18.0` while current Testbox proof requires `>= 0.22.0`.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
  On `origin/main`, `extensions/opencode-go/openclaw.plugin.json` only carries 2 manifest model rows (`deepseek-v4-pro` and `deepseek-v4-flash`), which means sync callers have no bundled metadata for rows like `mimo-v2.5-pro` and fall through to the default 200K budget described in #92912.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm install`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/context.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/reply-state.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.static-catalog.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/agents/context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-state.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.static-catalog.test.ts extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm build`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json --shell -- "corepack pnpm test src/agents/context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-state.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.static-catalog.test.ts extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose"`
- `node --input-type=module` manifest inspection commands for the post-fix rows and before/after coverage shown above

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `src/agents/context.test.ts` now asserts sync provider lookups can use bundled `refreshable` manifest rows for `opencode-go/mimo-v2.5-pro`.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-state.test.ts` now asserts memory-flush callers inherit the bundled `opencode-go` manifest limit for `mimo-v2.5-pro`.
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.static-catalog.test.ts` now covers `includeRefreshableDiscovery: true` for read-only metadata fallbacks.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Issue #92912 showed `opencode-go` models outside the 2-row manifest subset falling back to the default 200K sync context window.
- In this local checkout, validation commands are blocked by missing linked dependencies and an outdated Crabbox binary as described above.

If no test was added, why not?

Tests were added; automated execution is currently blocked by the local environment.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No

What is the highest-risk area?

The highest-risk area is sync model metadata resolution order: the new bundled manifest fallback must stay behind explicit config and cache hits so it does not override provider-specific runtime state for other providers.

How is that risk mitigated?

The fallback only runs after config scan, qualified cache lookup, bare cache lookup, and implicit qualified retry all miss, and it is limited to explicit-provider calls. The change also adds focused tests for the new `refreshable` read path and keeps the provider runtime catalog and manifest catalog on one shared data source.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review of the sync fallback order and the manifest/runtime catalog unification, then land if the proof limitations are acceptable or after re-running validation in a hydrated/Testbox-capable environment.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

The only remaining gap is automated execution in a fully hydrated environment with working `vitest`/`esbuild`/`json5` links and a Crabbox/Testbox binary new enough to run current remote proof.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

This PR intentionally supersedes the narrower onboarding-only fix shape discussed in #92913 by fixing the sync runtime path that affects already-configured users.

target.title: gateway hangs at `[gateway] starting...` when a declared provider lacks credentials (regression v2026.4.8 → v2026.4.26)
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix classification: Root-cause fix for a gateway startup lifecycle hang caused by unbounded implicit runtime provider catalog discovery.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High. The failing regression reproduced the missing timeout at the provider discovery boundary, and the behavior test verifies a never-resolving catalog is skipped after the bounded timeout.
- Root cause: implicit runtime provider catalog discovery violated the resolver contract because it only applied the 15s timeout in live-test mode; normal gateway startup callers without an explicit `providerDiscoveryTimeoutMs` passed `timeoutMs: null`, so a provider catalog that never resolved could keep `resolveImplicitProviders` pending and block gateway sidecar startup.
- Why this is root-cause fix: the patch moves the bounded default into the shared provider catalog timeout resolver, so every implicit runtime catalog run gets either the caller's explicit timeout or the 15s default. That addresses the source wait instead of special-casing OpenAI, Copilot, Kubernetes, probes, or credential names.
- What did NOT change: provider credential policy, fallback-model selection, config schema, migrations, Kubernetes probe behavior, static catalog handling, and caller-supplied timeout behavior remain unchanged.
- Intentionally out of scope: adding a new config knob or changing how uncredentialed providers are selected for runtime use after startup.
- Review focus: the default timeout contract in implicit discovery and the regression coverage for a never-resolving runtime catalog.

## Linked context

Addresses #90886

Related context: the issue reports `openclaw gateway run --bind custom --allow-unconfigured` hanging at `[gateway] starting...` when `models.providers` declares fallback providers whose credentials are absent. ClawSweeper pointed to the implicit provider catalog path as the source-backed unbounded wait and recommended a default bounded timeout without adding config surface.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: gateway startup should no longer be able to wait indefinitely on implicit runtime provider catalog discovery for a declared provider that cannot complete discovery.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw source runtime test harness for provider discovery and gateway startup, plus a Docker-level gateway startup proof against the current PR checkout. Linked issue live proof in the external Kubernetes environment is unavailable here; completed local validation and Docker-level verification are included below.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/models-config.providers.implicit.discovery-scope.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-startup.test.ts`
  - `git diff --check`
  - `pr-auto bounded-live-proof -- bash docker-gateway-startup-proof.sh` (`proof=provider=google with openai/copilot credentials absent; expected route is bounded implicit provider catalog discovery instead of an unbounded startup wait`)
- Evidence after fix:

```text
$ pr-auto bounded-live-proof -- bash docker-gateway-startup-proof.sh
Exit code: 0

healthz: HTTP 200 after 9s

Relevant gateway log lines:

```

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/models-config.providers.implicit.discovery-scope.test.ts
Exit code: 0

 RUN  v4.1.7 [redacted-local-path]

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  10 passed (10)
   Duration  7.49s (transform 3.14s, setup 514ms, import 5.07s, tests 1.62s, environment 0ms)
```

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-startup.test.ts
Exit code: 0

 RUN  v4.1.7 [redacted-local-path]

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  10 passed (10)
   Duration  21.10s (transform 12.11s, setup 2.13s, import 46ms, tests 18.14s, environment 0ms)
```

```text
$ git diff --check
Exit code: 0
<no output captured>
```

- Observed result after fix: `proof=provider=google` with OpenAI/Copilot credentials absent exercises the expected route through bounded implicit provider catalog discovery; runtime provider catalog discovery now receives a 15s default timeout even outside live-test mode, a never-resolving runtime catalog is skipped after that timeout, and the Docker gateway proof reached HTTP `/healthz` 200 instead of staying at `[gateway] starting...`.
- What was not tested: a live Kubernetes/AKS sidecar using real Google credentials and missing OpenAI/Copilot credentials.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: external Kubernetes access is unavailable here. The Docker proof used a fake proof-only Google key and skipped channel sidecars to keep the run scoped to gateway/model startup; no real model request was made, and no OpenAI/Copilot credentials were passed to the container.
- Before evidence: the new regression test failed before the fix with `expected null to be 15000`, matching the unbounded discovery path called out in the issue.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/models-config.providers.implicit.discovery-scope.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-startup.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Regression coverage added:

- `resolveImplicitProviders` now asserts runtime provider catalog discovery gets the 15s default timeout when no explicit timeout is supplied.
- A fake-timer regression test covers a never-resolving runtime catalog and verifies implicit provider resolution completes with that provider skipped.

What failed before this fix: the default-timeout regression failed with `expected null to be 15000`, matching the unbounded discovery path called out in the issue.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes`)

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`No`)

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes`)

What is the highest-risk area?

Provider catalog discovery may skip a provider whose runtime catalog takes longer than 15s when the caller has not provided a narrower or wider timeout.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The change only bounds implicit runtime catalog discovery; static catalogs and explicit provider config remain available.
- Existing explicit `providerDiscoveryTimeoutMs` callers still control their own timeout.
- The live discovery env override still applies in live mode.
- Timeout handling uses the existing warning-and-skip behavior rather than introducing a new failure mode or config surface.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review after CI.

Still waiting on: optional maintainer or reporter confirmation from a live Docker/Kubernetes reproduction, if required.

Bot or reviewer comments addressed: ClawSweeper's source-backed recommendation to add a default bounded timeout for implicit provider catalog discovery without new config surface.

target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: when sandbox mutation helpers fail with `python3: not found`, users only see a raw shell error and cannot quickly recover.
- Why it matters: write/edit flows fail with opaque errors, which looks like silent tool breakage and blocks normal agent workflows.
- What changed: mutation execution paths now translate missing-`python3` failures into actionable guidance that tells users to rebuild/update or configure a sandbox image with Python.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no mutation protocol changes, no Docker image build logic changes, and no change to successful write/edit behavior.

target.title: fix: add clear separator between metadata and user message
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** When a user message is passed to the LLM, the metadata blocks (sender info, conversation info, etc.) and the actual user message content are joined with only double newlines. This causes the LLM to sometimes misinterpret the message structure, treating the metadata as the entire message and ignoring the actual user content ("message is empty" replies).
- **Why it matters:** Users intermittently get incorrect "message is empty" responses even though their message was correctly stored in session history.
- **What changed:**
  - `runtime-context-prompt.ts`: When inbound metadata is present, `buildCurrentInboundPrompt()` injects `---openclaw:user-msg---` separator between the context prefix and the user prompt.
  - `strip-inbound-meta.ts`: Add `stripUserMessageSeparator()` post-processing in both `stripInboundMetadata` (webchat/API/gateway) and `stripLeadingInboundMetadata` (TUI) to remove the separator after metadata blocks are stripped. Gated on `didStripMetadata` flag for defense-in-depth.
  - `strip-inbound-meta.test.ts`: 7 new tests covering separator stripping and legacy preservation.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No API changes, no new types, no changes to `buildInboundUserContextPrefix`, no metadata format changes.

### Why `---openclaw:user-msg---` instead of `---\n**User Message:**`

The separator is a program-generated marker that **cannot exist** in any legacy stored message or user-typed content. This eliminates the need for complex detection logic (`_sep` markers, `seenUserContent` flags, structural JSON parsing) and makes legacy message compatibility trivial: the stripper only removes `---openclaw:user-msg---`, which legacy data never contains.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

target.title: feat(irc): support markdown messages via draft/multiline
target.body:
## Summary

Adds support for sending markdown-formatted messages over IRC using the `draft/multiline` CAP extension. This preserves newlines in bot responses, allowing IRC clients to properly render markdown content.

## Motivation

OpenClaw bots typically generate markdown-formatted responses (lists, code blocks, paragraphs). Without multiline support, these get flattened into single lines, making them unreadable on IRC.

The `draft/multiline` spec (https://ircv3.net/specs/extensions/multiline) allows sending multiple lines as a single BATCH, which modern IRC clients can display with proper formatting.

## Changes

- **CAP negotiation**: Requests `draft/multiline` capability on connect, waits for ACK before sending
- **Outgoing BATCH**: Messages with newlines are sent as `BATCH +id draft/multiline #channel` with tagged PRIVMSGs
- **Incoming BATCH**: Multi-line messages received via BATCH are combined into single payload
- **Control chars**: Newlines (`0x0a`) preserved (previously stripped as IRC control chars)
- **Configurable limits**: `textChunkLimit` defaults to 16384 (per spec recommendation)

## Compatibility

- **Servers without draft/multiline**: Falls back to flattening newlines (existing behavior)
- **Clients without draft/multiline**: Will see lines individually (no worse than before)
- **Backwards compatible**: No config changes required

## Testing

Tested on Ergo IRC server with `draft/multiline` support. Bot responses with markdown (lists, code blocks) render correctly in supporting clients.

target.title: fix(delivery-queue): skip stale first-attempt entries during startup recovery
target.body:
## Problem

When the gateway restarts, the delivery queue replays pending entries from previous runs. This is the correct behavior for messages that genuinely failed to send (network errors, provider downtime, etc.).

However, messages that were *enqueued but not yet sent* when the gateway stopped — due to an intentional restart or graceful shutdown — are also replayed. These entries have `retryCount === 0` and no `lastAttemptAt`, making them indistinguishable from crash survivors. The result: stale, out-of-context messages delivered to users long after the conversation that generated them.

**Example:** A user session generates 5 outbound messages. The gateway restarts before they're sent. On startup, recovery replays all 5 — delivering content from the previous session into the current one.

## Solution

Add a staleness check in `recoverPendingDeliveries`: first-attempt entries (`retryCount === 0`, `lastAttemptAt` undefined) older than `maxEntryAgeMs` (default: 10 minutes) are moved to `failed/` instead of being replayed.

Entries with `retryCount > 0` are unaffected — they are legitimate retry candidates regardless of age (a message that failed to send should keep retrying).

The check can be disabled by passing `maxEntryAgeMs: 0` to `recoverPendingDeliveries`.

## Changes

- `delivery-queue-recovery.ts`: add `DEFAULT_MAX_ENTRY_AGE_MS`, `skippedStale` field to `RecoverySummary`, `maxEntryAgeMs` opt to `recoverPendingDeliveries`, staleness check in recovery loop
- `delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts`: update existing `toEqual` assertions for new field, add 2 new tests covering stale skip and retried-entry bypass

## Tests

All 13 tests pass (`pnpm exec vitest run src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts`).

target.title: feat: thread sessionKey/sessionId into ProviderPrepareExtraParamsContext
target.body:
## Problem

OpenClaw never sets the `user` field in LiteLLM completion requests. This means all interactive sessions appear blank in LiteLLM spend logs — impossible to distinguish Telegram vs Discord traffic, or attribute cost spikes to a specific session/channel.

Example of the problem in LiteLLM `/spend/logs`:
```
end_user: ''   model: claude-sonnet-4-6   # is this Telegram? Discord? no way to know
end_user: ''   model: claude-sonnet-4-6
```

## Solution

Add `sessionKey` and `sessionId` to `ProviderPrepareExtraParamsContext` so that provider plugins can forward session identity to downstream proxies.

With this change, a custom provider plugin (or a future built-in behavior) can inject `user: sessionKey` into the completion request body:

```typescript
// In a custom provider plugin:
prepareExtraParams(ctx) {
  if (ctx.sessionKey) {
    return { ...ctx.extraParams, user: ctx.sessionKey };
  }
}
```

This would produce attributable spend logs:
```
end_user: agent:main:telegram:direct:571820863   model: claude-sonnet-4-6
end_user: agent:main:discord:channel:1480539453  model: claude-sonnet-4-6
```

## Changes

- **`src/plugins/types.ts`**: Add `sessionKey?: string` and `sessionId?: string` to `ProviderPrepareExtraParamsContext` with JSDoc
- **`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/extra-params.ts`**: Thread `sessionKey`/`sessionId` through `resolvePreparedExtraParams` and `applyExtraParamsToAgent`  
- **`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts`**: Pass `params.sessionKey` and `params.sessionId` at the `applyExtraParamsToAgent` call site

## Non-breaking

Both fields are optional. No existing behavior changes. Providers that don't use them are unaffected.

target.title: feat(control-ui): add cron calendar timeline view
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a 24h timeline strip to the Cron Jobs page in the Control UI, providing an at-a-glance view of today's scheduled tasks.

Closes #40644

## Screenshots

### Dark Theme — Full View
![Full page dark](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xvchris/cron-calendar-screenshots/main/dark-full.jpg)
*Summary strip with always-on chip, 24h timeline with NOW indicator, cluster badges, color-coded dots*

### Cluster Hover — Overlapping Jobs
![Cluster hover](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xvchris/cron-calendar-screenshots/main/cluster-hover.jpg)
*Hovering the ④ cluster at 22:00 shows 4 overlapping jobs with color-coded dots and times*

### Single Dot Hover — Job Info Popup
![Dot hover](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xvchris/cron-calendar-screenshots/main/dot-hover.jpg)
*Hovering oss-scan dot shows schedule, status, duration, and "Click → view run history" hint*

### Light Theme
![Light theme](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xvchris/cron-calendar-screenshots/main/light-theme.jpg)
*Full light theme support via existing CSS variables*

## Features

### Timeline Strip
- **24-hour timeline track** with grid lines and time labels
- **NOW indicator** — red vertical line showing current time, auto-updates every 60s
- **Color-coded dots** — each job gets a distinct color; green for past (ok), blue pulse for future (pending)
- **Cluster badges** — nearby dots (within 2.5%) auto-merge into a numbered badge; hover to see the full list
- **Hover popups** — JS-driven mouseenter/mouseleave with 150ms debounce for reliable hover behavior
- **Click navigation** — past dots jump to Run History (filtered), future dots scroll to job details
- **Zoom controls** — 24h (full day), Work (08:00–20:00), Now±3h (centered on current time)

### Always-On Chips
- High-frequency jobs (every <10min or cron `*/N` where N≤10) are displayed as chips in the summary strip instead of cluttering the timeline
- Hover to see: View Details / View History / Run Now actions
- Click to jump to run history

### Other
- **Mobile fallback** — timeline hidden on narrow screens, replaced by a simple text list of upcoming jobs
- **Full i18n** — all strings use `t()`, translations added for en, zh-CN, zh-TW, de, es, pt-BR
- **Theme support** — uses only existing CSS variables, works in both dark and light modes
- **No existing features removed** — purely additive changes to the view layer

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `ui/src/ui/helpers/cron-timeline.ts` | **New** — Timeline computation (zoom, clustering, high-freq detection) |
| `ui/src/ui/views/cron.ts` | Added timeline rendering between summary and workspace |
| `ui/src/styles/components.css` | Added timeline CSS (523 lines) |
| `ui/src/i18n/locales/*.ts` | Added `cron.timeline.*` keys to all 6 locales |

## Testing

- Computed timeline data from existing `CronProps` — no controller changes needed
- All new code is additive; existing view functions untouched
- TypeScript strict mode compatible

---

*AI Disclosure: This PR was authored with AI assistance (Claude). All code was reviewed and tested by a human.*

target.title: fix(gateway): forward child session node events to spawnedBy subscribers
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: gateway node subscriptions are keyed by exact `sessionKey`, so child-session node events disappear when only the parent session is subscribed.
- Why it matters: spawned agent chat activity can look dead from the gateway/node subscriber side even while work is actually running.
- What changed: added a direct-miss fallback in `createNodeSubscriptionManager.sendToSession()` that walks `spawnedBy` lineage, canonicalizes ancestor keys, and routes to the first ancestor with subscribers; added a focused regression test and changelog entry.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not change ACP execution, session transcript bridging, or broader subagent progress/status surfaces.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #34852
- Related #34905

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Spawned child-session node events now reach parent-session node subscribers when only the parent session is subscribed.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local dev checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): gateway node subscriptions
- Relevant config (redacted): default test harness

### Steps

1. Subscribe a node to a parent session key.
2. Emit a node event under a spawned child session key whose `spawnedBy` points to that parent.
3. Observe delivery through the node subscription manager.

### Expected

- Parent subscriber receives the child-session event.

### Actual

- Before this fix, delivery stopped on the child key miss and no event was sent.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: child-session events fall back through `spawnedBy` lineage to a subscribed parent; canonicalized parent lookup works; repeated sends reuse the cached ancestor lookup.
- Edge cases checked: no direct subscriber regression; partial object assertion accounts for `payloadJSON`; focused gateway test suite passes locally.
- What you did **not** verify: full gateway end-to-end node streaming against a live spawned-agent session.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this PR to restore exact-key-only subscription routing.
- Files/config to restore: `src/gateway/server-node-subscriptions.ts`, `src/gateway/gateway-misc.test.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: node subscribers receiving unexpected ancestor-routed events beyond the intended `spawnedBy` chain.

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: lineage fallback could repeatedly touch session storage on hot miss paths.
  - Mitigation: fallback only runs on direct misses, uses a bounded depth, and caches the resolved ancestor subscriber key.

Supersedes the dirty branch state in #34905.

target.title: fix: auto-compaction fires on fresh cached token counts (#66520)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Bug**: `runPreflightCompactionIfNeeded` returned early when `totalTokensFresh === true` without checking the compaction threshold, so auto-compaction never triggered for sessions with fresh token counts — even at 153% of the context window
- **Root cause**: The early-return optimization (skip transcript estimation when fresh data is available) accidentally bypassed the threshold comparison entirely
- **Fix**: Restructure token resolution so fresh persisted totals (which include `cacheRead` from Anthropic prompt caching) are projected forward and checked against `contextWindow - reserveTokens - softThreshold` before deciding whether to compact

## Details

When Anthropic's prompt cache absorbs nearly all tokens (100% hit rate), `derivePromptTokens` correctly computes `input + cacheRead + cacheWrite` (e.g. 99 + 305,000 + 0 = 305,099), and this is persisted as `totalTokens` with `totalTokensFresh: true`. However, `runPreflightCompactionIfNeeded` had this logic:

```typescript
if (!shouldUseTranscriptFallback) {
    return entry;  // BUG: skips threshold check entirely
}
```

The fix moves the threshold check into both branches (fresh and stale), using the fresh persisted value directly when available.

## Edge cases verified

- **100% cache hit (Anthropic)**: totalTokens=305k, contextWindow=200k — compaction now fires
- **0% cache hit**: Stale tokens fall through to transcript estimation path — behavior unchanged
- **Partial cache**: 180k total with 166k threshold — fires correctly
- **Below threshold**: 50k total with 166k threshold — no compaction, as expected
- **Heartbeat/CLI**: Still skipped regardless of token count
- **Non-Anthropic providers**: No change — providers without caching have `cacheRead=0`, so `totalTokens` is just `input + cacheWrite`, same as before

## Test plan

- [x] `shouldRunPreflightCompaction` unit tests: 100% cache hit triggers, below threshold skips, partial cache boundary correct
- [x] `runPreflightCompactionIfNeeded` integration tests: fresh tokens above threshold trigger compaction, below threshold skip, stale tokens use transcript fallback, heartbeat skips
- [x] All existing compaction tests pass unchanged
- [x] Session usage persistence tests pass (65 tests)
- [x] Followup runner tests pass (19 tests)
- [x] Preemptive compaction tests pass (9 tests)

Fixes #66520

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: feat(cron): add agent-turn mode for failure alerts
target.body:
Closes #56521

## What

Adds a new `agent-turn` mode for cron job `failureAlert` that injects the error as a system event into the agent session instead of sending an outbound notification (Telegram/etc).

## Why

When cron jobs fail, the current `announce` mode sends a notification to the user's chat. But the agent (main session) is unaware of the failure — the human has to relay the error manually.

With `agent-turn` mode, the failure alert triggers the agent directly via `enqueueSystemEvent` + `requestHeartbeatNow`, allowing the agent to:
- Diagnose the error autonomously
- Attempt a fix if possible
- Report the outcome to the human

## How

- New `failureAlert.mode: 'agent-turn'` option
- In `emitFailureAlert`, when mode is `agent-turn`, bypasses `sendCronFailureAlert` and uses the existing `enqueueSystemEvent` + `requestHeartbeatNow` path
- CLI: `openclaw cron edit <id> --failure-alert-mode agent-turn`

## Usage

```bash
openclaw cron edit <job-id> --failure-alert-mode agent-turn
```

Or in config:
```json
{
  "failureAlert": {
    "mode": "agent-turn",
    "after": 1
  }
}
```

target.title: config: support ${VAR:-default} env var fallbacks
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Config values using `${VAR}` env substitution have no way to specify a fallback when the var is unset — they either throw `MissingEnvVarError` or get swallowed by the `onMissing` warning path, leaving a literal `${VAR}` placeholder in the resolved config.
- **Why it matters:** Common deployment patterns (Docker, CI, local dev) need sane defaults for optional env vars without crashing the gateway. This is standard shell syntax (`${VAR:-default}`) that users already expect to work.
- **What changed:**
  - `env-substitution.ts`: Extended `parseEnvTokenAt` to parse `:-` separator inside `${...}` tokens, added `defaultValue` field to `EnvToken`, and updated `substituteString` to use the default when the var is missing/empty — bypassing both the `onMissing` callback and the `MissingEnvVarError` throw.
  - `env-preserve.ts`: Updated `hasEnvVarRef` regex and `tryResolveString` parser to recognize `${VAR:-default}` so config write-back round-trips correctly restore the original template instead of persisting the resolved value.
  - Tests: 11 new test cases across both files covering defaults with URLs, empty defaults, colons in values, inline usage, nested structures, `onMissing` bypass, and write-back restoration.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No new files, no new dependencies, no changes to the config loading pipeline or integration points. The `containsEnvVarReference` helper required no changes (already works because it checks `kind === "substitution"`). Uppercase-only var name restriction is preserved. Escape syntax (`$${VAR}`) is unaffected.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related: N/A (new feature, no existing issue)
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

N/A — new feature.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `src/config/env-substitution.test.ts`, `src/config/env-preserve.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - `${VAR:-default}` resolves to default when var is unset or empty
  - `${VAR:-default}` resolves to env value when var is set
  - `${VAR:-}` resolves to empty string
  - Defaults with special characters (URLs, colons, paths) work correctly
  - `onMissing` callback is NOT triggered when a default is present
  - Config write-back (`restoreEnvVarRefs`) correctly round-trips `${VAR:-default}` templates
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The substitution is a pure string transform with well-defined inputs; unit tests at the function level cover all code paths.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Config files now support `${VAR_NAME:-default_value}` syntax in any string field. When the env var is unset or empty, the default value is used instead of throwing an error. Examples:

```json5
{
  models: {
    providers: {
      "custom-gateway": {
        baseUrl: "${GATEWAY_URL:-https://api.example.com/v1}",
        apiKey: "${API_KEY}"  // no default — still required
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
${VAR} with VAR unset -> MissingEnvVarError (or onMissing warning)
${VAR:-default} -> not recognized, treated as literal text

After:
${VAR} with VAR unset -> MissingEnvVarError (unchanged)
${VAR:-default} with VAR unset -> "default"
${VAR:-default} with VAR=x -> "x"
${VAR:-} with VAR unset -> ""
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No — default values are static strings from the config file, not from external input. The resolution path is identical to existing `${VAR}` substitution.
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS Darwin 25.2.0
- Runtime/container: Node 22+, pnpm
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: N/A

### Steps

1. Add `${MISSING_VAR:-https://fallback.example.com}` to any string field in openclaw config
2. Do NOT set `MISSING_VAR` in environment
3. Load config

### Expected

- Value resolves to `https://fallback.example.com`

### Actual

- Before: `MissingEnvVarError` or unresolved placeholder
- After: Resolves to `https://fallback.example.com`

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- All 43 tests pass (23 env-substitution + 20 env-preserve), including 11 new tests for default syntax
- `pnpm check` passes (lint, typecheck, format)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: All new test cases, full `pnpm check` gate, full `pnpm test` on both touched test files
- Edge cases checked: Empty defaults, colons in default values, inline mixed usage, nested objects, `onMissing` bypass, write-back round-trip
- What you did **not** verify: Full Docker e2e (planned next — testing in production Docker container)

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes — existing `${VAR}` syntax is unchanged. `${VAR:-...}` was previously not recognized and would have been treated as a literal string (not matching the uppercase-only var name pattern due to `:-`), so no existing configs are affected.
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

target.title: fix(doctor): suppress false group-allowlist warning when all accounts override groupAllowFrom (fixes #92684)
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: `openclaw doctor` over-warns about an empty top-level `groupAllowFrom` when every sub-account defines its own populated `groupAllowFrom`. The warning claims group messages will be silently dropped, but at runtime `account.groupAllowFrom ?? parent.groupAllowFrom` means the empty top-level fallback is never consulted.
- **Root Cause**: `scanEmptyAllowlistPolicyWarnings` in `empty-allowlist-scan.ts` unconditionally scans the top-level channel config as if it were an account, without checking whether sub-accounts already override `groupAllowFrom`. Both the shared default group-allowlist warning path and the channel plugin `extraWarningsForAccount` path were un-gated.
- **Fix**: Before scanning the top-level record, check whether every active sub-account already supplies its own `groupAllowFrom`. If all accounts override it, suppress both the default group-allowlist warning hook and the `extraWarningsForAccount` plugin hook for the top-level call (the skip is applied only when `parent` is undefined, so account-level checks are unaffected). The existing per-account scan with parent-fallback resolution still catches accounts that genuinely lack a populated allowlist.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan.ts` — added pre-scan of sub-account `groupAllowFrom`, wrapped both `shouldSkipDefaultEmptyGroupAllowlistWarning` and `extraWarningsForAccount` hooks when all accounts override
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - DM-policy (`dmPolicy: "allowlist"`) handling — the top-level DM warning is still emitted; only the group-allowlist false positive is addressed
  - Channel plugin custom skip hooks for account-level calls — the wrapped hooks delegate to the original for non-top-level calls
  - Any runtime message-delivery logic — this is a doctor advisory fix only
  - `allowFrom`-as-group-coverage accounting — the channel's `groupAllowFromFallbackToAllowFrom` heuristic is left to future work; the current fix is conservative and only suppresses when every account has an explicit `groupAllowFrom` list

### Reproduction

1. Configure a channel with `groupPolicy: "allowlist"`, empty top-level `groupAllowFrom`, and two or more accounts each with a populated `groupAllowFrom`.
2. Run `openclaw doctor`.
3. **Before this PR**: doctor emits a top-level group-allowlist warning claiming group messages will be dropped, even though every account overrides the fallback and inbound group messages are processed normally.
4. **After this PR**: the top-level group-allowlist warning is suppressed when all accounts supply their own `groupAllowFrom`; warnings still fire when no accounts exist or when some accounts lack `groupAllowFrom` (requiring fallback to the empty top-level).

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed (92684)**: doctor `EmptyAllowlistCheck` warns the top-level record as if it were an account when `groupPolicy: "allowlist"` and `groupAllowFrom` is empty, even though every sub-account defines its own `groupAllowFrom`. The fix suppresses that false positive on both the shared default warning path and the channel plugin extra-warning path, while preserving the warning when accounts genuinely lack a populated allowlist.

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22 — direct scanner invocation loaded through the real `createChannelDoctorEmptyAllowlistPolicyHooks` framework (no mocks, not a Vitest test)

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node --import tsx scripts/direct-scanner-proof-92684.mjs`

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
=== Real scanner proof: #92684 (direct, non-test invocation) ===
Config: telegram groupPolicy=allowlist, top-level groupAllowFrom=[]
        work.groupAllowFrom=["@friend1"], personal.groupAllowFrom=["@friend2"]

Total warnings: 2
Top-level group false positives: 0
Result: ✅ No false positive — all accounts override groupAllowFrom

Regression (no accounts): top-level group warnings = 1 ✅ still warns
```

**Observed result after fix**: direct scanner invocation with the real doctor hooks framework confirms zero top-level group-allowlist false positives when both accounts supply their own `groupAllowFrom`. The two remaining warnings are account-level Telegram first-time-setup advisories (not false positives), and the regression check confirms the top-level warning still fires when no accounts exist to override it.

**What was not tested**: live `openclaw doctor` CLI invocation against a fully provisioned OpenClaw installation was not driven — the scanner is exercised directly through the real channel-doctor hooks framework rather than through the full CLI pipeline.

**Repro confirmation**: the direct scanner script fails on origin/main (emits 1 top-level group-allowlist false positive through the Telegram extra-warning path) and succeeds after the third patch commit (emits 0 top-level group false positives, gating both the shared and extra-warning paths).

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: the wrapped `shouldSkipDefaultEmptyGroupAllowlistWarning` and `extraWarningsForAccount` hooks are restored immediately after the top-level `checkAccount` call, but the function mutates `params` directly.
  **Mitigation**: original function references are saved before the wrap and unconditionally restored on the next line, so account-level checks always receive the original hooks.
- **Risk**: a channel with a mix of accounts where some have `groupAllowFrom` and others do not could lose the top-level warning.
  **Mitigation**: the suppress condition requires **every** active sub-account to have a populated `groupAllowFrom` — a single account without it keeps both hook paths intact.
- **Risk**: a channel that supports `groupAllowFromFallbackToAllowFrom` might have accounts whose `allowFrom` provides effective group coverage without an explicit `groupAllowFrom`.
  **Mitigation**: this is deferred to future work; the current fix is conservative — it only suppresses when every account has an explicit `groupAllowFrom` list, so no valid warning is lost.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CLI / doctor commands
- [x] Commands (doctor shared)

### Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-policy.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan-92684.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/preview-warnings.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/repair-sequencing.test.ts`

### Review Findings Addressed

- **[P2] Gate the Telegram extra-warning path** — fixed in 7538369: `extraWarningsForAccount` is now wrapped alongside `shouldSkipDefaultEmptyGroupAllowlistWarning`, suppressing the top-level call when all accounts override. Confirmed by direct scanner proof (0 false positives vs 1 on main).
- **[P2] Count account allowFrom fallback coverage** — deferred; the current fix is conservative (requires explicit `groupAllowFrom` on every account). Extending to `allowFrom`-as-group-coverage requires channel-specific `groupAllowFromFallbackToAllowFrom` awareness and is left to future work.

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #92684

target.title: fix(qqbot): allow scoped sandbox media sends
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: sandbox-enabled agents can produce media paths such as `/workspace/...`, but QQBot outbound sends dropped the scoped media access passed by core and rejected those files before reaching the QQBot API.
- Solution: thread core-provided scoped media roots through the QQBot adapter and outbound media engine while keeping existing root containment checks.
- What changed: QQBot outbound text/media paths now preserve `mediaAccess`/`mediaLocalRoots`, and regression tests cover direct media sends plus `<qqmedia>` tags from scoped local roots.
- What did NOT change: no broad host-file access was added, no QQBot credential/config shape changed, and WebChat production code was not changed.

Fixes #92827

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: sandbox-created files referenced as `/workspace/...` can be sent/staged through WebChat and QQBot paths instead of being blocked by local media-root checks.

Real environment tested: Linux local OpenClaw gateway, PR head `2a447ab`, isolated runtime config, QQBot enabled with live credentials supplied through an environment SecretRef.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
node openclaw.mjs gateway run --port 19772 --bind loopback --auth none --verbose --force
node openclaw.mjs message send --channel qqbot --target "qqbot:c2c:<redacted>" --message "OpenClaw PR #92872 real-recipient QQ text proof sha=2a447ab"
node openclaw.mjs message send --channel qqbot --target "qqbot:c2c:<redacted>" --message "OpenClaw PR #92872 real-recipient QQ media proof sha=2a447ab" --media "pr-92872-qqbot-proof.txt"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts

Observed result after fix: OpenClaw resolved and sent the outbound QQBot text and media messages to a real C2C recipient captured from the live QQBot gateway; both sends returned QQBot message IDs. This covers the successful delivery path in addition to the sandbox media-root regression coverage.

What was not tested: group-chat QQBot delivery was not exercised; this proof is scoped to C2C QQBot delivery and WebChat media staging for the issue path.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test plus local runtime proof
- Target test file: `extensions/qqbot/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts`, `extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts`

target.title: fix(reply): suppress direct tool-error progress leaks
target.body:
## Summary
- suppress text-only tool error progress in direct chats when the turn still produces a normal final reply
- keep the existing terminal fallback path intact so real failures still surface when there is no usable final answer
- add a regression test covering the reproduced Telegram DM cold-start leak

## Repro / proof
Concrete live repro from Joseph's Telegram DM on **June 3, 2026** after the machine had been off for about a week:

1. Power the system back on.
2. Start a direct Telegram conversation with a lightweight greeting like `Yo`.
3. The agent startup sequence tries to read expected daily memory files for today/yesterday.
4. If one of those files does not exist, the internal tool failure is emitted into the DM before or alongside the real reply.

Observed visible output:
- user: `Yo`
- assistant final reply: `Yo.`
- leaked tool-progress error: `⚠️ 🛠️ print lines 1-220 from memory/2026-06-03.md (agent) failed`

That is the wrong surface behavior for a trivial greeting. The user should get the greeting, and any internal failed read should stay internal unless the turn actually ends in failure.

## Root cause
`dispatch-from-config` was forwarding text-only `onToolResult(... isError: true ...)` payloads directly to user-visible delivery in direct chats. That bypassed the later terminal-warning policy, which already tries to avoid showing warning text when a normal reply exists.

## Fix
This change suppresses direct-chat text-only tool error progress payloads before delivery, while preserving the existing end-of-turn fallback logic. The result is:
- normal direct replies stay clean
- tool failures can still surface at the end of a truly failed turn
- media/approval payloads are unaffected
- verbose `full` mode behavior is unchanged

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Direct Telegram chats could leak text-only internal tool-error progress, such as a missing startup memory-file read, before/alongside a normal final reply. This patch keeps the normal final reply visible while suppressing that raw internal progress line.
- **Real environment tested**: Exact PR head `1579b5197f3938f6ec904f3e319c7514e7f88291` checked out from `jewseppi/openclaw` in `/tmp/openclaw-pr89975-proof.6sEupH/repo` on Joseph's OpenClaw host, using `pnpm 11.2.2` and the repository lockfile. A live Telegram Bot API dispatcher was wired into that exact-head `dispatchReplyFromConfig` path and sent to Joseph's real Telegram DM (`chat_id=6300969793`). The live Telegram direct conversation also reproduced the same missing-memory-file internal failure mode during this proof attempt without surfacing the raw `sed: can't read memory/2026-06-06.md` / `memory/2026-06-05.md` tool output as Telegram chat text.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash

git -C /tmp/openclaw-pr89975-proof.6sEupH/repo rev-parse HEAD
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm test -- --run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts

```

- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal capture from the exact-head checkout and live Telegram dispatch probe:

The exact-head regression coverage also proves the direct-chat dispatch path drops the text-only `isError` tool-progress payload and still sends the normal final reply (`Yo.`), while the embedded-runner coverage preserves real terminal warning fallback behavior for unacknowledged/unsafe failures.
- **What was not tested**: I could not produce maintainer-owned native Telegram Desktop Mantis video from this account. `workflow_dispatch` for `mantis-telegram-desktop-proof.yml` is repository-admin-only, and previous comment-trigger attempts were skipped/unauthorized because `jewseppi` only has read-level access on `openclaw/openclaw`.

## Test
- `pnpm test -- --run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `pnpm test -- --run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`

## Related evidence
This came up during a broader cold-start recovery path after the machine had been powered off for days. There was also a separate stale-`dist` cold-start failure reported earlier in the week. That looks like a different root cause; this PR only fixes the leaked internal tool-error symptom above.
